now i want ot convert itnto the code ui page giveem emprompt

ChatGPT said:

Use this master prompt with Cursor AI, Claude Code, Lovable, v0.dev, Bolt.new, Windsurf, or ChatGPT to convert the UI into production-quality code.

Master Prompt

Act as a Senior UI/UX Designer, Senior Frontend Developer, and Next.js Expert.

Analyze the provided homepage design image and recreate it as a pixel-perfect, production-ready website.

Do not redesign or simplify anything. Match the design as closely as possible while writing clean, reusable, maintainable code.

Tech Stack

  • Next.js 15 (App Router)

  • React 19

  • TypeScript

  • Tailwind CSS v4

  • Framer Motion

  • Lucide React Icons

  • shadcn/ui components

  • next/image for optimized images

Requirements

General

  • Pixel-perfect recreation

  • Fully responsive

  • Mobile First

  • Semantic HTML

  • Accessible (WCAG)

  • SEO Friendly

  • Reusable components

  • Clean folder structure

  • No inline styles

  • Proper TypeScript types

  • Optimized performance

Layout Structure

app/
│
├── layout.tsx
├── page.tsx
│
components/
│
├── Navbar.tsx
├── Hero.tsx
├── Stats.tsx
├── Services.tsx
├── Portfolio.tsx
├── WhyChooseUs.tsx
├── Process.tsx
├── Testimonials.tsx
├── CTA.tsx
├── Footer.tsx
│
data/
constants/
types/
public/

Animations

Use Framer Motion.

Include

  • Fade Up

  • Fade Left

  • Fade Right

  • Scale

  • Stagger Children

  • Image Reveal

  • Scroll Trigger

  • Counter Animation

  • Button Ripple

  • Hover Lift

  • Navbar Scroll Animation

Performance

  • Use next/image

  • Lazy Loading

  • Code Splitting

  • Dynamic Imports

  • Lighthouse Score 95+

  • CLS < 0.1

  • Optimized Images

Accessibility

  • Keyboard Navigation

  • Proper aria-labels

  • Semantic HTML

  • Focus States

  • Screen Reader Support

Deliverables

Generate a fully functional Next.js project, including:

  • Complete folder structure

  • Reusable React components

  • Tailwind CSS styling

  • Framer Motion animations

  • Responsive layouts for mobile, tablet, and desktop

  • TypeScript interfaces

  • Clean, maintainable code

  • No placeholders or incomplete sections

The final output should match the provided design as closely as possible while following modern frontend best practices and production-ready coding standards.


You said:

Got it! I'll change the page background. Which background would you like? 1. A deep navy or charcoal tone 2. A subtle dark gradient Or describe what you have in mind!

ChatGPT said:

For a luxury photography website, I recommend Option 2: a subtle dark gradient. It complements wedding and event photography, makes images stand out, and gives the site a premium feel.

Use this specification:

CSS
Background:
linear-gradient(
  180deg,
  #0B0B0B 0%,
  #121212 35%,
  #1A1A1A 70%,
  #0B0B0B 100%
);

Accent Color:
#C8A165 (Luxury Gold)

Cards:
rgba(255,255,255,0.04)
backdrop-filter: blur(10px);

Borders:
rgba(200,161,101,0.2)

Primary Text:
#FFFFFF

Secondary Text:
#CFCFCF

Or simply tell the AI:

Use a luxurious cinematic dark gradient background throughout the website. Blend charcoal black (#0B0B0B), deep graphite (#121212), and rich dark brown (#1A1A1A) with soft golden highlights (#C8A165). The background should feel elegant, premium, and timeless, ensuring the photography remains the primary focus. Avoid flat colors and use subtle radial gradients with a soft vignette effect for depth.
